skill.name: c4-context
skill.description: Creates C4 System Context documentation, including high-level system descriptions, personas, user journeys, external dependencies, and Mermaid context diagrams.
skill.summary: Create stakeholder-friendly C4 System Context architecture documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, C4 terminology, documentation links, and repeated system-context wording as possible security patterns. Manual review found these are false positives in a documentation-only skill, with no shell execution, weak cryptography use, reconnaissance logic, data exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear system context before deeper architecture design. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce stakeholder-friendly C4 context diagrams and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Drafts short and long system descriptions for C4 context documents.
Identifies human users, programmatic users, and external systems.
Maps key features to personas and user journeys.
Creates Mermaid C4Context diagrams with people, systems, databases, and relationships.
Links context-level documentation to container and component documentation.
Keeps output readable for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
limitations: Requires accurate product, user, and dependency information from the user.
Does not inspect source code or infrastructure automatically.
Does not validate Mermaid rendering in an external diagram tool.
Focuses on C4 context level, not container, component, or code-level detail.

use_cases.title: Document a New Product
use_cases.description: Create a first C4 System Context view before architecture planning or stakeholder review.

use_cases.title: Align Engineering Stakeholders
use_cases.description: Summarize users, external systems, and high-level relationships in one shared architecture document.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Reviews
use_cases.description: Convert existing notes into a structured context document with personas, journeys, and dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Context Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first system overview.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create C4 System Context documentation for this system: [system name]. Include the main users, external systems, and a Mermaid C4Context diagram.

prompt_templates.title: Document Personas and Journeys
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on users and workflows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Identify personas and user journeys for [system name]. Show which features each persona uses, then connect them to a C4 System Context diagram.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Existing Architecture Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using existing documentation as input.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these architecture notes to create C4 System Context documentation. Include system descriptions, personas, features, external dependencies, and diagram relationships: [notes].

prompt_templates.title: Review and Improve Context Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for quality review and refinement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this C4 System Context document for missing users, unclear system boundaries, weak relationship labels, and unnecessary technical detail: [document].

output_examples.input: Create context documentation for a customer support portal.
output_examples.output: A stakeholder-friendly document with a portal summary, customer and agent personas, support workflows, external CRM and email dependencies, and a Mermaid context diagram.

output_examples.input: Analyze my notes for missing C4 context elements.
output_examples.output: A gap list covering missing personas, unclear external systems, absent journey steps, weak relationship names, and recommended documentation updates.

output_examples.input: Turn container and component docs into a context-level overview.
output_examples.output: A simplified system context view that removes low-level implementation detail and highlights users, adjacent systems, and business-facing relationships.

best_practices: Provide the system purpose, user groups, and known integrations before asking for the diagram.
Keep relationship labels short and understandable for non-technical stakeholders.
Review the output against C4 context scope before adding container or component details.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for detailed deployment architecture.
Do not include databases, queues, or APIs unless they are external to the system boundary.
Do not ask for final diagrams without providing enough system and stakeholder context.

faq.question: What C4 level does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports the C4 System Context level, which is the highest-level view of a software system.

faq.question: Can it create Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides Mermaid C4Context diagram text that can be rendered by compatible Mermaid tools.

faq.question: Does it generate container or component diagrams?
faq.answer: No. It can link to those documents, but its output stays focused on system context.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the system purpose, users, important features, external systems, and any existing architecture notes.

faq.question: Is this useful for non-technical audiences?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes stakeholder-friendly language and avoids low-level implementation details.

faq.question: Can it validate the architecture automatically?
faq.answer: No. It structures and reviews documentation, but humans must verify accuracy and completeness.
